text: **Anna:** Hi Ashley!
**Ashley:** Hey Anna!
**Ashley:** How was your {weekend|週末|/ˈwiːkˌend/|I had a great weekend.}?
**Anna:** It was great.
**Anna:** I went to the DC State {Fair|祭り/見本市|/fɛr/|We visited the county fair.}
**Ashley:** I would have gone, but I was {busy|忙しい|/ˈbɪzi/|I am busy with work.}
**Ashley:** How was it?
**Anna:** It was {awesome|素晴らしい|/ˈɔːsəm/|That concert was awesome!}
**Anna:** I won first place in a {pet|ペット|/pɛt/|My pet cat is very friendly.} contest.
**Ashley:** But you don't have a pet, do you?
**Anna:** Yes and no.
**Anna:** What do you mean by {pet|ペット|/pɛt/|I love my pet dog.}?
**Anna:** A pet is an animal that lives with you.
**Anna:** People love their pets.
**Ashley:** Ashley said, You don't have a pet, do you?
**Anna:** She used a {tag|タグ/小さな|/tæɡ/|Add a tag to the question.} question, a small question at the end of a sentence.
**Anna:** Let's look for more of them.
**Anna:** Lock a pet!
**Anna:** Who's this cute little {dog|犬|/dɔɡ/|My dog loves to play.}?
**Ashley:** Oh right, you haven't met my dog, have you?
**Ashley:** This is Dublin.
**Anna:** Hi Dublin, nice to meet you.
**Anna:** He's so cute.
**Ashley:** Thanks
**Ashley:** So tell me about the state fair.
**Anna:** There were the usual things, fair food, cute babies, music, but
**Anna:** There must have been a {dozen|12個/多数|/ˈdʌzən/|I bought a dozen eggs.} different contests, and some were pretty strange.
**Anna:** There was a contest to see who could {spit|つばを吐く|/spɪt/|Don't spit on the ground.}
**Anna:** a watermelon seed the farthest.
**Anna:** You should have seen all the people who wanted to spit a watermelon seed.
**Anna:** And it was {pretty|とても/かなり|/ˈprɪti/|She is pretty smart.} fun to watch.
**Anna:** But Ashley, the {pet|ペット|/pet/|My pet is a dog.} contests were the best.
**Anna:** First, there was a pet {parade|パレード|/pəˈreɪd/|The parade was colorful.}
**Anna:** Lots of people walked with their dogs.
**Ashley:** That sounds so fun, but Anna, tell me, how did you win a pet contest?
**Ashley:** Without a pet.
**Anna:** Okay, the closest thing I have is my pet rock
**Anna:** I know it's {silly|馬鹿げた/おかしな|/ˈsɪli/|That joke is silly.}, but I've had it since I was a little girl.
**Anna:** So for a {joke|冗談|/dʒoʊk/|He told a funny joke.}, I brought it to the fair.
**Anna:** But then I was {invited|招待された|/ɪnˈvaɪtɪd/|I was invited to the party.} to enter the {one-of-a-kind|唯一の/特別な|/wʌn əv ə ˈkaɪnd/|This is a one-of-a-kind gift.} pet contest.
**Anna:** And I won!
**Ashley:** Anna, only you could have won a pet contest with a pet rock.
**Ashley:** I should have brought Dublin.
**Ashley:** He would have won a contest.
**Anna:** It's not very easy to win one of these.
**Ashley:** Does he have a costume?
**Anna:** They had a {costume|衣装/コスチューム|/ˈkɒstjuːm/|She wore a beautiful costume.} contest.
**Anna:** You should have seen this little girl.
**Anna:** She and her dog wore {matching|同じ/マッチする|/ˈmætʃɪŋ/|They wore matching shirts.} princess costumes.
**Ashley:** Dublin doesn't wear costumes.
**Anna:** Does he know any {tricks|芸/トリック|/trɪks/|Dogs can learn many tricks.}?
**Ashley:** Come this way.
**Ashley:** Now, watch this
**Ashley:** Oh my god
**Anna:** You caught it, Dublin!
**Anna:** Oh, and you {killed|殺した/成功した|/kɪld/|He killed the target.} it!
**Ashley:** Oh!
**Anna:** Good dog!
**Anna:** Ashley, he could have won the best {catch|捕まえる/キャッチ|/kætʃ/|Can you catch the ball?} contest
**Anna:** Oh, good boy.
**Anna:** Ashley, {pets|ペット/動物|/pets/|I love my pets.} are fun, aren't they?
**Anna:** Yes.
**Ashley:** And they become part of your {family|家族|/ˈfæmɪli/|My family is very close.}
**Anna:** Ashley?
**Anna:** I think I want a {dog|犬|/dɔɡ/|I love my dog.}
**Anna:** No, I know I want a dog.
**Ashley:** It's a big {responsibility|責任|/rɪˌspɒnsəˈbɪləti/|Responsibility is important.}
**Anna:** You may want to {spend|過ごす/費やす|/spend/|I spend time with friends.} time with a dog first.
**Anna:** That is a great idea.
**Anna:** But where can I find a dog?
**Ashley:** You know, Anna, I have some {shopping|買い物|/ˈʃɒpɪŋ/|I go shopping every weekend.} to do
**Ashley:** Do you want to watch Dublin for a couple of hours?
**Anna:** Yes, I would.
**Anna:** I will take very good {care|世話/注意|/ker/|Take care of yourself.} of him.
**Ashley:** I know you will.
**Anna:** Here's his {leash|リード/引き紐|/liːʃ/|Put the dog on a leash.}
**Anna:** Thank you.
**Anna:** Okay, I'll miss you.
**Anna:** Aww, I don't want you to be {lonely|孤独な|/ˈloʊnli/|He feels lonely today.}
**Anna:** Here, Rocky can stay with you.
**Ashley:** Anna, I really don't need okay.
**Ashley:** Thanks, Anna.
**Anna:** Sure.
**Anna:** Bye, Ashley.
**Anna:** Come on, Dublin.
**Anna:** Bye, Rocky.
**Anna:** Hi!
**Anna:** Anna's rock is {cute|かわいい|/kjuːt/|That puppy is cute.}, isn't it?
**Anna:** Oh, there is another {tag|タグ/付け札|/tæɡ/|Write a tag on the item.} question.
**Anna:** You found some more, didn't you?
**Ashley:** You haven't {met|出会った/会った|/met/|I met my best friend last year.} my {dog|犬|/dɒɡ/|My dog loves to play fetch.}, have you?
**Anna:** Go to the VOA Learning English {website|ウェブサイト|/ˈwebsaɪt/|I found useful information on this website.} to learn more.
